meaning: 鍝堝皵绉戝か[鑻忚仈涔屽厠鍏颁笢鍖楅儴鍩庡競]
examples: A city of southern European U. S. S. R. on the Dnieper River south-southwest of Kharkov.
He decided instead to establish control of the main road running eastwards from Kiev to Kharkov, the best route from Poland, so as to secure his supply line.
For Kanevsky, who was born in Kharkov, Ukraine, the path from obscure expat to sought-after artist has been a winding one.
Simone was born in Kharkov, Ukraine (then the Soviet Union) to Russian parents, but came to the U.S. when she was one.
They came from a little hamlet in the Kharkov district in order to establish an entirely new farming colony here, in the heart of the untamed landscape of the forsaken Menashe highlands.
